# AGENTS.md

<!-- generated by Project Spine; edit `brief.md` or rerun `spine compile` rather than hand-editing -->

**Project Spine** — Launch a marketing site for Acme Payroll's new SMB product line within 6 weeks.

**Stack:** next • typescript • tailwind

**Project type:** `saas-marketing`

> Source of truth: `.project-spine/spine.json` (hash `8976dc15b57e7f67`). Every rule below has a traceable source. If a rule is wrong, fix the upstream input (`brief.md`, repo state, or `design-rules.md`) and rerun `spine compile`.

## Repo conventions

- Use the Next.js App Router (`app/`). Prefer server components by default; only opt into client components via `"use client"` when needed.
- Style exclusively with Tailwind utility classes. Do not add inline `style` props or ad-hoc CSS unless the component is outside the design system.
- TypeScript strict mode is on. Never use `any`; prefer `unknown` + narrow at the boundary.

## Do / Don't

- Prefer small, focused diffs. One concern per PR or patch.
- Always show the exact test or typecheck command you ran and its result.
- When following a rule from this file, cite it by section heading so reviewers can audit.
- Do not refactor code unrelated to the task, even if it looks better.
- Do not add new dependencies without flagging the choice and reason.
- Prefer server components and static rendering for content pages; lean on revalidation for pricing.
- Do not introduce client-only JS for above-the-fold content; it blocks LCP.
- Do not add third-party tracking without privacy review — note the vendor and data sent in the PR.

## Never do this (unsafe)

- Never modify generated files (anything under `dist/`, `build/`, `.next/`, or marked `@generated`).
- Never edit lockfiles manually.
- Never commit secrets. Redact any `.env*` content that appears in logs or diffs.
- Never hardcode pricing numbers in templates; reference a single source (MDX frontmatter or CMS).

## File placement

- Pages go in `app/`. Route segments use `page.tsx`. Shared UI goes in `components/` or `src/components/`.

## Response expectations

- Before claiming work is complete, run the repo's typecheck and test commands and show the output.
- If the brief is ambiguous on a specific section, ask rather than guess. Cite the section heading.

## Component & design rules

- Hero — full-width headline, sub-copy, primary + secondary CTA, optional product image.
- FeatureGrid — responsive grid of feature cards with icon, title, description.
- LogoStrip — customer logos row with grayscale treatment and alt text.
- Testimonial — quote block with avatar, name, role, company.
- PricingTable — tier cards with features list, highlight recommended tier, keyboard navigable.
- FAQ — accordion with proper ARIA state.
- CTABanner — full-width conversion block for mid-page and footer.

## Accessibility baseline

- All interactive elements must be reachable and operable with the keyboard alone.
- Focus must be visible at all times; never remove outlines without replacing them.
- Text contrast must meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text).
- Every form control has a programmatic label.
- Pages use proper landmark regions (header, main, nav, footer) and a sensible heading order.
- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` for any non-essential animation.
- Images decorative vs informative is explicit; informative images have real alt text.
- Hero headline uses a single h1; no competing h1s on the page.

## UX rules

- Every page has a primary CTA above the fold; secondary CTAs never compete in weight.
- Every interactive element exposes hover, focus-visible, active, and disabled states.
- Exactly one primary CTA per page, above the fold; secondaries must visually yield.
- Copy passes the Hemingway 'ops lead can skim in 30s' bar on long-form pages.

## QA guardrails

- `tsc --noEmit` must pass on every PR.
- Success criterion to verify before launch: Homepage, 3 product pages, pricing, /compliance, /security, 2 case studies live.
- Success criterion to verify before launch: 99th-percentile page weight <250KB on mobile.
- Success criterion to verify before launch: All interactive elements keyboard-operable and screen-reader labeled.
- Success criterion to verify before launch: 40+ qualified trial signups in the first month.
- LCP under 2.0s on mobile (p75), CLS under 0.1, INP under 200ms.
- Total transferred page weight under 250KB on /, /product, /pricing at mobile viewport.
- Every CTA path tested from keyboard; no click-only interactions.
- Forms validate inline and expose errors to screen readers via aria-live.

## What we are building (from brief)

- Launch a marketing site for Acme Payroll's new SMB product line within 6 weeks.
- Generate qualified trial signups from SMB founders in the US and UK.
- Replace the legacy WordPress site with a repo-native, versionable build.
- Pass Lighthouse a11y ≥95 on all key pages.

## Constraints

- Stack must be Next.js 15 (app router) + Tailwind. Infra is already on Vercel.
- Content managed in MDX. No CMS for the MVP.
- Design system tokens ship as a JSON file from the product team — reference only, do not fork.
- Legal review required on every claim about pricing or compliance.

## Known risks

- Tokens JSON may change mid-build; we need to absorb updates without rewriting components.
- Ops lead audience reads long-form; the marketing team is pushing for short copy — tension to manage.
- Legal turnaround is 48h; compliance-adjacent copy blocks can bottleneck launch.

## Open warnings

There is 1 unresolved warning in `warnings.json`. Agents should review them before starting a new capability.
